project: jleechanclaw Phase 10 — Slack user-token post + minimax orchestration loop
description: Phase 8: iterate /copilot + CodeRabbit re-review loop until all PR comments resolved and CodeRabbit approves. Skeptical subagent reviews test quality.
exitCriteria: Skeptical subagent reviews src/tests/ and outputs REVIEW_PASS
PR #10 has no unresolved CRITICAL or BLOCKING comments
CodeRabbit latest review is 'Approved' or has 0 actionable comments
PR description tracking table updated with all comments addressed

context.coderabbit_trigger: post a comment on PR #10 tagging @coderabbitai[bot] asking it to re-review
context.review_criteria: verify tests are non-trivial, cover real logic, have assertions, use proper mocking

userStories.title: Skeptical subagent review of test suite
userStories.description: Launch a code-review subagent to review src/tests/ thoroughly. The subagent must: 1) read all test files in src/tests/ 2) verify tests have real assertions (not just pass or empty bodies) 3) verify mocks are set up correctly (returncode, side_effect, etc.) 4) verify test isolation (env patching with clear=True) 5) confirm 262 tests all pass via PYTHONPATH=src python -m pytest src/tests/ --tb=short -q. Output REVIEW_PASS or REVIEW_FAIL with specific evidence. Only mark passes=true when subagent outputs REVIEW_PASS.
acceptanceCriteria: No empty test bodies (just pass with no assertions)

userStories.title: Post /copilot reply and update PR tracking table
userStories.description: Run the /copilot workflow on PR #10: 1) fetch all comments with commentfetch 2) categorize by severity 3) verify recent direct fixes (commit 19e2cbe7a) cover: RQ_REDIS_URL, .gitignore, ralph.sh codex path, watch-ralph.sh stat portability, agent_registry atomic writes + lock, run_e2e.py live stub 4) generate responses.json with FIXED/ACKNOWLEDGED status for each resolved comment 5) post consolidated [AI responder] summary comment on PR #10 6) update PR description with tracking table. Commit message must use [copilot] prefix.
acceptanceCriteria: Consolidated [AI responder] comment posted on PR #10
All CRITICAL/BLOCKING comments have commit evidence

userStories.title: Ask CodeRabbit to re-review and confirm approval
userStories.description: Post a comment on PR #10 that: 1) tags @coderabbitai[bot] 2) states that all actionable comments have been addressed (list the commit hashes) 3) asks CodeRabbit to do a fresh review. Then wait for CodeRabbit response (poll gh pr reviews every 30s, up to 5 minutes). If CodeRabbit still has actionable comments, fix them and loop. If CodeRabbit approves or has 0 actionable comments, mark passes=true.
acceptanceCriteria: CodeRabbit latest review state is APPROVED or comment count is 0 actionable
No new CRITICAL/BLOCKING comments from CodeRabbit

userStories.title: Verify Phase 9 bug fixes pass full test suite
userStories.description: Run PYTHONPATH=src python -m pytest src/tests/ --tb=short -q. Confirm 262+ tests pass. Check that test_agent_registry.py covers lookup() and list_all() with concurrent access, test_heartbeat_bridge.py expects _post called every invocation (not cached), and test_task_poller.py counts only successful dispatches. If any test fails, fix the code and re-run until green.
acceptanceCriteria: No import errors or collection errors

userStories.title: Trigger CodeRabbit re-review of Phase 9 bug fix commit
userStories.description: Post a GitHub comment on PR #10 that: 1) tags @coderabbitai[bot] 2) lists the Phase 9 bug fixes from commit ce49fb06 (lock on lookup/list_all, fd sentinel, heartbeat cache removed, dispatch bool return) 3) asks CodeRabbit to re-review. Then run: gh pr checks 10 --repo jleechanorg/jleechanclaw to confirm CodeRabbit status. If CodeRabbit flags new issues, fix them. Mark passes=true when CodeRabbit shows SUCCESS.
acceptanceCriteria: gh pr checks shows CodeRabbit state=SUCCESS
No new CRITICAL comments from CodeRabbit

userStories.title: Post test message to #worldai as jleechan via user token
userStories.description: Use curl to POST to https://slack.com/api/chat.postMessage with Authorization: Bearer $SLACK_USER_TOKEN. Channel: C0AH3RY3DK6. Message: '<@U0AEZC7RX1Q> user-token test from jleechan — please confirm you see this and reply'. Verify response JSON has ok:true and message.user == U09GH5BR3QU. Mark passes=true only when confirmed.

userStories.title: Trigger minimax claude orchestration via Slack and confirm it runs
userStories.description: Post a message to #worldai as jleechan (using SLACK_USER_TOKEN env var via curl) tagging @openclaw: '<@U0AEZC7RX1Q> orchestrate a single minimax agent: run PYTHONPATH=src python -m pytest src/tests/ --tb=short -q in /Users/jleechan/project_jleechanclaw/jleechanclaw and post the result back here'. Then poll gh pr checks or slack channel history (GET https://slack.com/api/conversations.history?channel=C0AH3RY3DK6&limit=10 with bot token) every 30s for up to 5 minutes to see if openclaw responds with test results. Mark passes=true when openclaw posts a reply containing test count or PASSED.
acceptanceCriteria: openclaw bot replies in #worldai within 5 minutes
Reply contains test results or confirmation

userStories.title: Scaffold Amazon clone site in /tmp/amazon-clone
userStories.description: Create /tmp/amazon-clone/ directory and write all 5 files: index.html (homepage with product grid), product.html (product detail page), cart.html (shopping cart page), style.css (Amazon-style: dark #131921 top nav, search bar, orange buy button, product cards with stars), app.js (products array with 8 items, addToCart/removeFromCart using localStorage, search filter, cart badge counter, renderProducts function). Each product must have: id, name, price, rating (1-5), reviewCount, imageUrl (use placeholder.com), category. index.html must import style.css and app.js. Run verification: ls -la /tmp/amazon-clone/ must show all 5 files, wc -l /tmp/amazon-clone/*.html must show 20+ lines each.
acceptanceCriteria: ls /tmp/amazon-clone shows index.html, product.html, cart.html, style.css, app.js
app.js contains products array with 8+ items

userStories.title: Verify Amazon clone is complete and functional
userStories.description: Verify the /tmp/amazon-clone site is complete. Check: 1) grep -c 'addToCart' /tmp/amazon-clone/app.js returns 1+. 2) grep 'localStorage' /tmp/amazon-clone/app.js must find cart persistence. 3) grep 'search\|filter' /tmp/amazon-clone/app.js must find search logic. 4) index.html must have a search input and product grid div. 5) cart.html must display cart items and subtotal. Post a summary to #worldai as jleechan: 'Amazon clone built at /tmp/amazon-clone/ — N files, products: [list 3 product names]'. Use curl with $SLACK_USER_TOKEN environment variable to channel C0AH3RY3DK6.
